PHOTON-VAGUE: Advanced Photonic Solutions for Industry

1. Excellence

1.1 Objectives

The project aims to advance the state of the art in photonic sensing. We will develop new sensors and apply them to industrial contexts. The work will be innovative and go beyond current knowledge.

1.2 Methodology

We will use standard spectroscopic methods and apply machine learning to analyse the results. Experiments will be conducted at the partner facilities. The results will be validated and published.

2. Impact

2.1 Expected outcomes and impacts

The project will have significant impact on European industry. SMEs and large companies will benefit from the results. The technology will be commercialised after the project ends.

2.2 Dissemination and exploitation

Results will be published in international peer-reviewed journals and presented at conferences. The consortium will exploit the results commercially.

3. Implementation

3.1 Work plan

The project is organised into two work packages.

Work Package 1: Research and Development
This work package covers all research activities. The team will conduct experiments, analyse data and develop prototypes.

Work Package 2: Dissemination
This work package covers all communication and dissemination activities.

The project will last 36 months. Deliverables will be agreed during the project.

3.2 Consortium

The consortium consists of two partners:

Partner 1: ACME Research Institute — a well-known research organisation with expertise in photonics and spectroscopy.

Partner 2: University of Example — a leading European university with strong research capabilities.

The partners have complementary expertise and will work together effectively throughout the project.
